title: Run lint for mac builds
body:
Run golangci-lint for mac jobs but ignore all failures temporarily until they're fixed.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note

```

comments.body: Cockpit tests failed for commit d0f2d2a2413e6a130bc5c3dd114327c2cc0339d4. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

Unrelated to this PR, but that's the second time I've seen a [rawhide test run](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/dfe6c212-adef-400f-8357-4943d17755fd/) time out on `podman build`. This is a trivial Containerfile (just a FROM and a RUN), it really shouldn't take two minutes. That may be a regression in podman:next or the rawhide kernel, etc. Something to watch out for.

@martinpitt presumably you're talking about `RuntimeError: Timed out on 'podman build -t localhost/test-intermediate /tmp/tmp.ABfI0DuVUL'`?   I don't know anything about these tests unfortunately.  Is there someone you can recommend that should be altered to this?
@baude @edsantiago this is ready for an initial look.  It's not perfect or pretty but should get the job done.

Force-push: Addressed all/most of Eds concerns.

In case it gets lost, [Ed's general question was](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21372#discussion_r1469838534):

    [AT]containers/podman-maintainers, why is there no "remote" in the `for` loop? Does that have 
    anything to do with the `TODO` on line 30?

    FWIW I ran `remote` on my laptop and it passed.
[lint log](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5286078056235008/logs/lint.log). Will someone be working on those?

> Will someone be working on those?

My understanding is yes, the intent is to get them fixed up at some point in the near(?) future.  I can't say for certain whom is doing that.  I got a "SGTM" from Brent Re: `|| true` ignoring the results for now.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
IMHO, this is ready to merge.
